questions.text: What is the stated purpose of revealing the Book (Quran) in Verse 1?
questions.answer: To bring mankind out of darknesses into the light by permission of their Lord.

questions.text: Why does Allah send every Messenger in the language of his people?
questions.answer: So that he may make the message clear to them.

questions.text: What was the common reaction of the nations to their Messengers throughout history?
questions.answer: They put their hands to their mouths in rejection and expressed deep doubt about what they were called to.

questions.text: What is the famous promise of Allah regarding gratitude in Verse 7?
questions.answer: If you are grateful, I will surely increase you; but if you deny, My punishment is severe.

questions.text: How did the Messengers respond to the threats of being expelled from their lands?
questions.answer: They relied upon Allah, stating that they would be patient with the harm inflicted upon them.

questions.text: Describe the drink given to the tyrants in Hell.
questions.answer: They will be given 'Sadeed' (festering water/puss) which they will gulp but hardly be able to swallow.

questions.text: What will Satan say to his followers after the matter has been decided on Judgment Day?
questions.answer: He will say Allah gave them a true promise, while he gave a false one; he had no authority over them except that he invited them and they responded.

questions.text: To what does Allah compare a 'Good Word' (Kalimah Tayyibah)?
questions.answer: To a good tree whose root is firmly fixed and whose branches reach into the sky, yielding fruit at all times.

questions.text: To what does Allah compare an 'Evil Word' (Kalimah Khabithah)?
questions.answer: To an uprooted tree from the surface of the earth, having no stability.

questions.text: How does Allah keep the believers firm in this life and the next?
questions.answer: With the 'word that stands firm' (the Shahadah/Faith).

questions.text: What has Allah subjected (Sakh-khara) for the benefit of humans mentioned in this Surah?
questions.answer: The ships, the rivers, the sun, the moon, and the night and day.

questions.text: What is the consequence if humans try to count the favors of Allah?
questions.answer: They will never be able to number them.

questions.text: What was Ibrahim's (AS) primary prayer for the city of Makkah?
questions.answer: To make the city secure and to keep him and his sons away from worshipping idols.

questions.text: Why did Ibrahim (AS) settle his family in an uncultivated valley?
questions.answer: So that they might establish prayer (As-Salat).

questions.text: In his prayer, how does Ibrahim (AS) describe Allah's knowledge of our emotions?
questions.answer: He says Allah knows what we conceal and what we declare.

questions.text: What was Ibrahim's (AS) request for his parents and the believers at the end of his prayer?
questions.answer: Forgiveness on the Day the account is established.

questions.text: Should the Prophet think that Allah is unaware of what the wrongdoers do?
questions.answer: No, Allah is only delaying them until a Day when eyes will stare in horror.

questions.text: What will be the physical state of the wrongdoers as they run on Judgment Day?
questions.answer: They will be racing ahead, necks outstretched, heads raised, their gaze not returning to them, and their hearts void.

questions.text: What will happen to the Earth and the Heavens on the Last Day?
questions.answer: The Earth will be replaced by another earth, and the Heavens as well.

questions.text: What is the final summary of the Quran's message in the last verse?
questions.answer: It is a notification (Balagh) for mankind that they may be warned and know that He is one God.
